title: Object-Oriented Programming Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of object-oriented programming concepts

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a pillar of Object-Oriented Programming?
questions.explanation: The four main pillars of OOP are Encapsulation, Inheritance, Polymorphism, and Abstraction. Compilation is not a pillar of OOP.

options: Defining multiple methods with the same name but different parameters
Redefining a method in a subclass
questions.explanation: Method overloading allows multiple methods with the same name but different parameter lists in the same class.

questions.questionText: What is the difference between method overriding and method overloading?
options: No difference, they are the same
Overriding changes method signature, overloading doesn't
Overloading is compile-time polymorphism, overriding is runtime polymorphism
questions.explanation: Method overloading is resolved at compile-time (static polymorphism), while method overriding is resolved at runtime (dynamic polymorphism).

options: Hiding internal implementation details and exposing only necessary methods
questions.explanation: Encapsulation is the bundling of data and methods that operate on that data, while hiding internal implementation details from the outside.

questions.questionText: Which keyword is used to prevent method overriding in Java?
questions.explanation: The 'final' keyword prevents a method from being overridden in subclasses.

options: A class that cannot be instantiated directly
A class with only static methods
A class that inherits from multiple parents
questions.explanation: An abstract class cannot be instantiated directly and typically contains one or more abstract methods that must be implemented by subclasses.

questions.questionText: What is the main advantage of inheritance?
options: Code reusability and establishing IS-A relationships
questions.explanation: Inheritance promotes code reusability by allowing classes to inherit properties and methods from parent classes, and establishes IS-A relationships.

options: A HAS-A relationship where objects contain other objects
questions.explanation: Composition represents a HAS-A relationship where a class contains objects of other classes as instance variables.

options: Binding methods at runtime based on object type
questions.explanation: Dynamic binding (late binding) determines which method to call at runtime based on the actual object type, enabling polymorphism.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of access modifiers?
options: To control the visibility and accessibility of class members
questions.explanation: Access modifiers (public, private, protected) control the visibility and accessibility of class members, implementing encapsulation principles.
